intro: Bourreria bosseri is a species of plant in the family Boraginaceae, commonly known as the borage family, which is native to the island of Madagascar. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been associated with the reduction of pain and the treatment of various infections. The bioactive constituents of Bourreria bosseri include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which have been identified as responsible for its medicinal properties.
